replyValues.thread_ts: ={{ $('Parse Task Overview WP').first().json.thread_ts || $('Parse Task Overview WP').first().json.ts || $('Prepare Task Overview').first().json.thread_ts || $('Prepare Task Overview').first().json.ts }}

parameters.text:
=You are helping a Slack user with questions about ONE OpenProject work package (task).

USER'S CURRENT MESSAGE (you MUST answer this — it is the primary instruction):
{{ $json.userQuestion }}

RECENT CONVERSATION IN THIS SLACK CHANNEL (shared memory for the channel; OpenProject facts below are ground truth):
{{ $json.conversationContext }}

PRIORITY (OVERRIDES EVERYTHING BELOW):
- If the user asks for a specific **format** (bullet points, numbered list, table, headings, one paragraph, short vs long, etc.), follow it exactly.
- If they ask for a specific **language** (English, Urdu in Arabic script, or Roman Urdu), write the entire answer in that language.
- If they ask for both format and language, satisfy both.
- Only when they give no preference, use the default rules that follow.

DEFAULT LANGUAGE (when not specified): Mirror the language of their message (English, Urdu script, or Roman Urdu) when natural.

FACTS FROM OPENPROJECT (only use these; do not invent):
- Task ID: {{ $json.task_id }}
- Title: {{ $json.subject }}
- Status: {{ $json.status }}
- Assigned to: {{ $json.assignee }}
- Full description (read carefully for follow-up or detail questions):
{{ $json.description || 'No description provided.' }}

RESPONSE RULES (when the user did not specify a custom format):
1) **Broad / first-time questions** (e.g. "overview of this task", "what is task 123", "summarize") — Give a clear structured summary: title, assignee, what the work involves, current status.

2) **Specific or follow-up questions** (e.g. a feature name, metric, step, section, or "explain X") — **Do NOT** repeat the same generic overview. Read the **full description** and answer **only** what they asked, with **detail** grounded in the text. Expand on that aspect.

3) If the description does not contain what they asked, say so honestly and give only what is available.

4) Prefer **depth** when they ask for explanation of a part; prefer **brevity** when they only asked for a high-level overview.

Reply with Slack-ready text only (no JSON, no preamble).

If they gave no format or angle, consider whether they want a broad overview or a specific detail—then answer accordingly: for specific detail, lead with a direct answer; do not open with the same generic summary as for a broad overview.

CHATBOT: Memory is per Slack channel (everyone in the channel shares this transcript). Use RECENT CONVERSATION for follow-ups so the user need not repeat task names or details when they clearly continue the same topic. If they ask something completely different, answer that new topic. Facts must still match OpenProject data in this prompt.

parameters.name: filters
parameters.value: ={{ JSON.stringify([{ project: { operator: '=', values: [ $('Overview: Match Open Project').first().json.projectId ] } }]) }}

nodes.name: Not Delete Assignee Error1
nodes.notes: Error message when sender is not the task assignee (delete).

nodes.name: Is Delete Assignee?1
nodes.notes: TRUE → delete task. FALSE → send error.

nodes.name: Validate Delete Assignee1
nodes.notes: Checks if Slack sender is the assignee of the task (Delete branch).

nodes.name: Not InProgress Assignee Error1
nodes.notes: Error message when sender is not the task assignee (in_progress).

nodes.name: Is InProgress Assignee?1
nodes.notes: TRUE → set in progress. FALSE → send error.

nodes.name: Validate InProgress Assignee1
nodes.notes: Checks if Slack sender is the assignee of the task (In Progress branch). Supports multi-task loop.

nodes.name: Send Not Member Error to User1
Respond 200 OK Early1
nodes.notes: Respond 200 to Slack immediately to prevent retries.

parameters.text:
=ASSIGNEE TO MATCH: "{{ $json.assigneeName }}"

TOTAL USERS IN OPENPROJECT: {{ $json.totalUsers }}

COMPLETE LIST OF ALL OPENPROJECT USERS:
{{ $json.usersList }}

---

TASK: Find the single best matching user from the list above for the assignee name "{{ $json.assigneeName }}".

If the assignee value is empty, null, or only whitespace, immediately return: {"found": false, "userId": null, "userName": "", "userLogin": "", "reason": "No assignee provided"}

MATCHING RULES — apply in this priority order:
1. Exact full name match (case-insensitive).
2. Exact login match (case-insensitive).
3. Exact email match (case-insensitive).
4. First name or last name match (case-insensitive).
5. Partial/contains match.
6. Fuzzy/intelligent match.

CRITICAL RULES:
- ALL comparisons must be strictly case-insensitive.
- You MUST check EVERY single user in the list.
- NEVER invent or assume a user. Only select from the provided list above.
- The userId in your response MUST exactly match the integer id from the list.
- If absolutely no user matches, return found=false.

OUTPUT FORMAT — Return ONLY a valid JSON object:
{"found": true, "userId": <integer>, "userName": "<exact full name from list>", "userLogin": "<exact login from list>", "reason": "<one sentence>"}

nodes.name: IF Project Found
nodes.notes: TRUE → resolve assignee. FALSE → PROJECT_NOT_FOUND.

nodes.name: Switch
nodes.notes: Done / Delay / in progress / project_creation / reporting / delete_task / set_context / no_task / task_overview / project_overview / daily_update.

parameters.text:
==You are an intelligent intent classifier and structured data extractor for a project management bot connected to Slack.

You are given the following input data:

Text:
{{ $json.text }}

User ID:
{{ $json.userId }}

Your task is to carefully read the Text, understand what the person is trying to do, and return a structured JSON response.

---

INTENT CLASSIFICATION — pick exactly ONE of these:

1) "project_creation"
   The person wants to CREATE a new task or work package.
   Look for signals like: assign, create, add, make, schedule, give task, new task, handle this, take care of, etc.

2) "done"
   The person is saying a task is COMPLETE, FINISHED, or CLOSED.
   Look for signals like: done, completed, finished, closed, khatam ho gaya, delivered, submitted, it is done, I have completed, will be done before due date, etc.
   MUST reference at least one task ID. Supports single ID, multiple IDs (comma-separated), or a range (e.g. "285 to 306").

3) "delay"
   The person is saying a task is DELAYED, PENDING, or NEEDS MORE TIME.
   Look for signals like: delayed, not done yet, need more time, will take few more days, pending, late ho gaya, etc.
   MUST reference a task ID AND include a reason.

4) "set_in_progress"
   The person wants to CHANGE a task STATUS to In Progress.
   Look for signals like: set to in progress, change status to in progress, mark as in progress, in progress kar do, status change karo, it is new please set to in progress, etc.
   Supports single ID, multiple IDs (comma-separated), or a range (e.g. "285 to 306").
   MUST reference at least one task ID.

5) "delete_task"
   The person wants to DELETE or REMOVE one or more tasks or work packages.
   Look for signals like: delete, remove, hatao, drop, hata do, delete karo, remove task, etc.
   Supports single ID, multiple IDs (comma-separated), or a range (e.g. "285 to 306").
   MUST reference at least one task ID.

6) "set_context"
   The user is stating their current task or what they are working on today.
   Look for signals like: "my task today is", "I am working on", "today I will", "focused on", "handling this today", etc.
   MUST extract: task_id (if mentioned) or task description.
   Fields: task_id, task_description, context_type="active"

7) "no_task"
   The user has no tasks assigned for today.
   Look for signals like: "no task today", "I am free", "nothing assigned", "no work today", "available for new work", etc.
   Fields: context_type="no_task", reason (optional)

8) "project_overview"
   The user wants an OVERVIEW or SUMMARY of a PROJECT (not a task).
   Look for signals like: "overview of this project", "I need an overview of this project", "give me an overview of the project", "what is this project", "explain the project", "project summary", "what does this project do", "project objectives", etc.
   MUST extract the project name or identifier from the message (the name the user uses to refer to the project).
   If they only say "this project" without a name, set Project to "".

9) "task_overview"
   The user wants an OVERVIEW or SUMMARY of a TASK / WORK PACKAGE (not the whole project).
   Look for signals like: "overview of this task", "give me an overview of this task", "explain task", "what is task 123", "task #456 summary", "describe this task", etc.
   MUST extract at least one numeric task_id (work package ID) from the message.

10) "reporting"
   The person wants a PROJECT REPORT or STATUS UPDATE.
   Look for signals like: report, report do, status, progress, kitna kaam hua, project status, show report, weekly report, completion, how much done, etc.
   MUST reference a project name.

11) "daily_update"
   The user is giving a daily work update paragraph/reminder-style update, often without task ID.
   Examples: "I completed work yesterday", "still working, will finish tomorrow", "I forgot to send yesterday activity".
   MUST extract daily_status (done/delayed/in_progress), remarks (full message), and activity_date in YYYY-MM-DD.
   Convert relative words like yesterday/today/tomorrow into ISO date based on current date.

---

CRITICAL RULES:

- If message mentions "in progress" + task ID → ALWAYS "set_in_progress". Never "done" or "project_creation".
- If message says task is complete/done/finished + task ID → "done".
- If message asks to create/assign a task → "project_creation".
- If message mentions delay/pending/needs time + task ID + reason → "delay".
- If message asks to delete/remove a task + task ID → "delete_task".
- If the user asks for a project overview/summary (what the project does, objectives) without focusing on a single task ID → "project_overview" (not "task_overview").
- If the user asks for a task/work package overview and provides a task ID → "task_overview" (not "project_overview").
- If the user provides a day-wise activity paragraph without a clear task ID flow, classify as "daily_update".
- Language does not matter — English, Urdu, Roman Urdu, or mixed.

---

FIELD RULES:

FOR "project_creation":
- Task: concise title. NEVER leave empty — generate from context if needed.
- Project: project name from message.
- Assignee: person being assigned.
- StartDate: convert relative start dates to YYYY-MM-DD. Look for signals like: start from, starting, begins, shuru, se shuru karo. If not mentioned set to "".
- Due: convert relative dates to YYYY-MM-DD.
- Priority: Low, Normal, High, or Immediate. If not mentioned set to "".
- Description: any extra detail or context.
- task_id: null
- reason: null

FOR "done":
- task_id: extract the first numeric ID (for backwards compatibility).
- task_ids: array of ALL numeric IDs to close. If a range like "285 to 306" is given, expand to the full array [285,286,...,306]. If single ID, return [ID]. If comma-separated, return all.
- reason: null
- All other fields: "" or null

FOR "delay":
- task_id: extract numeric ID.
- reason: extract clearly from message.
- All other fields: "" or null

FOR "set_in_progress":
- task_id: extract the first numeric ID (for backwards compatibility).
- task_ids: array of ALL numeric IDs to set in progress. If a range like "285 to 306" is given, expand to the full array [285,286,...,306]. If single ID, return [ID]. If comma-separated, return all.
- reason: null
- All other fields: "" or null

FOR "delete_task":
- task_id: null
- task_ids: array of ALL numeric IDs to delete. If a range like "285 to 306" is given, expand to the full array [285,286,...,306]. If single ID, return [ID]. If comma-separated, return all.
- reason: null
- All other fields: "" or null

FOR "set_context":
- task_id: extract numeric ID if mentioned, otherwise null.
- task_description: extract what they are working on today.
- context_type: "active"
- text: include the original full message text.
- All other fields: "" or null

FOR "no_task":
- context_type: "no_task"
- reason: extract why they have no task (optional).
- text: include the original full message text.
- All other fields: "" or null

FOR "project_overview":
- Project: project name or identifier exactly as the user wrote it (used for lookup; casing may differ from OpenProject).
- Intent: "project_overview"
- task_id: null
- task_ids: []
- All other fields: "" or null

FOR "task_overview":
- task_id: the numeric work package ID (use the first ID if several are mentioned).
- task_ids: all numeric IDs if multiple are mentioned, else [task_id]
- Intent: "task_overview"
- Project: optional
- All other fields: "" or null

FOR "reporting":
- Project: project name from message.
- All other fields: "" or null

FOR "daily_update":
- activity_date: convert to YYYY-MM-DD (handle yesterday/today/tomorrow and explicit dates).
- **Use the real current calendar year** — never output old years (e.g. 2023) unless the user typed that exact date in their message.
- daily_status: one of done, delayed, in_progress.
- remarks: full user message text.
- task_id: null unless explicitly present.
- task_ids: [] unless explicitly present.

---

Set user_id EXACTLY equal to the given User ID above.
Do NOT infer it from text. Do NOT modify it.

---

Return ONLY valid JSON in this exact format, no markdown, no explanation:

{
  "Intent": null,
  "Project": "",
  "Task": "",
  "Description": "",
  "Assignee": "",
  "StartDate": "",
  "Due": "",
  "Priority": "",
  "task_id": null,
  "task_ids": [],
  "reason": null,
  "user_id": null,
  "text": "",
  "context_type": null,
  "task_description": null,
  "activity_date": "",
  "daily_status": "",
  "remarks": ""
}

nodes.name: Webhook Response OK1
nodes.notes: Immediately respond 200 OK to Slack. Must be under 3 seconds.

parameters.jsCode:
const data = $input.first().json;
return [{ json: data }];

nodes.name: Merge Voice & Text1
nodes.notes: Single entry point for AI Agent. Both voice (transcribed) and text arrive here.

nodes.name: check text1
nodes.notes: If no audio file → pass text forward. Otherwise output nothing.

nodes.name: Prepare Message Fields1
nodes.notes: Only DMs to bot reach here. Extract text, userId, audio info.

nodes.name: Skip: Channel Message1
nodes.notes: Silently ignore messages from public/private channels.

nodes.name: Is Direct Message to Bot?1
nodes.notes: TRUE → process (user DMed the bot app). FALSE → ignore channel message.

nodes.name: Skip: Bot Own Message1
nodes.notes: Silently ignore bot's own messages.

nodes.name: Is Bot Own Message?1
nodes.notes: TRUE → ignore (bot's own message). FALSE → check if DM.

nodes.name: Extract Event Info1
nodes.notes: Extracts channel_type and bot flags. is_dm=true only for Direct Messages to bot app.

nodes.name: Respond Challenge1
nodes.notes: Slack URL verification response. Must respond < 3s.

nodes.name: Is URL Verification?1
nodes.notes: TRUE → respond challenge to Slack. FALSE → process event.

nodes.name: Normalize Input1
nodes.notes: Flatten nested body so type/challenge/event fields are accessible.

nodes.name: Webhook
nodes.notes: Entry point. Receives all Slack event payloads.

parameters.name: calculate_report
parameters.description: Calculates and formats a PM report from work packages data. Parameters: work_packages_json (full JSON string from get_work_packages), project_name (string), date_range (e.g. 'Last 7 days'), report_type ('project' | 'individual' | 'both'). Returns Slack-formatted report.

nodes.name: Prepare Slack Reply1
nodes.notes: Extracts agent output and injects thread metadata for Slack reply

parameters.description:
Fetch project work package data.

Inputs (REQUIRED):
- project_name (string)
- days_back (number)

IMPORTANT:
You MUST pass inputs as structured JSON like:
{
  "project_name": "AI PM",
  "days_back": 7
}
